unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
/* * Source file for all OpenSSL-specific code for the TLS/SSL layer. No code * but vtls.c should ever call or use these functions. */
/* Wincrypt must be included before anything that could include OpenSSL. */
/* Undefine wincrypt conflicting symbols for BoringSSL. */
/* for the boundary function */
/* for the ssl config check function */
/* The last #include files should be: */

/* Uncomment the ALLOW_RENEG line to a real #define if you want to allow TLS renegotiations when built with BoringSSL. Renegotiating is non-compliant with HTTP/2 and "an extremely dangerous protocol feature". Beware.
#define ALLOW_RENEG 1 */
/* added in 1.1.0 -pre1 */
/* since 1.1.0 -pre3 */
/* since 1.1.0 -pre5 */
/* funny typecast define due to difference in API */
/* For OpenSSL before 1.1.0 */
/* nope */
/* 1.0.2 or later */
/* not present in older OpenSSL */

/* * Whether SSL_CTX_set_keylog_callback is available. * OpenSSL: supported since 1.1.1 https://github.com/openssl/openssl/pull/2287 * BoringSSL: supported since d28f59c27bac (committed 2015-11-19) * LibreSSL: supported since 3.5.0 (released 2022-02-24) */
/* Whether SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites is available. * OpenSSL: supported since 1.1.1 (commit a53b5be6a05) * BoringSSL: no * LibreSSL: supported since 3.4.1 (released 2021-10-14) */

/* * Whether SSL_CTX_set1_curves_list is available. * OpenSSL: supported since 1.0.2, see * https://www.openssl.org/docs/manmaster/man3/SSL_CTX_set1_groups.html * BoringSSL: supported since 5fd1807d95f7 (committed 2016-09-30) * LibreSSL: since 2.5.3 (April 12, 2017) */
/* ... but it is not the case with old versions of OpenSSL */
/* the function exists */
/* the functionality is not disabled */
/* * Whether the OpenSSL version has the API needed to support sharing an * X509_STORE between connections. The API is: * * `X509_STORE_up_ref` -- Introduced: OpenSSL 1.1.0. */
/* What API version do we use? */
/* !LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER */
/* !LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER */
/* HAVE_SSL_X509_STORE_SHARE */
unit.cpp:define: #define push_certinfo(_label, _num) \ do { \ long info_len = BIO_get_mem_data(mem, &ptr); \ Curl_ssl_push_certinfo_len(data, _num, _label, ptr, info_len); \ if(1 != BIO_reset(mem)) \ break; \ } while(0)

function.name: pubkey_show
asn1_object_dump

decl.name: X509V3_ext

function.name: Curl_ossl_certchain

unit.comment: /* quiche or OpenSSL */
/* USE_PRE_1_1_API */

function.name: ossl_bio_cf_create
ossl_bio_cf_destroy
ossl_bio_cf_ctrl
ossl_bio_cf_out_write
ossl_bio_cf_in_read

unit.decl_stmt: static BIO_METHOD ossl_bio_cf_meth_1_0 = { BIO_TYPE_MEM, "OpenSSL CF BIO", ossl_bio_cf_out_write, ossl_bio_cf_in_read, NULL, /* puts is never called */ NULL, /* gets is never called */ ossl_bio_cf_ctrl, ossl_bio_cf_create, ossl_bio_cf_destroy, NULL };

function.name: ossl_bio_cf_method_create
ossl_bio_cf_method_create
ossl_bio_cf_method_free

unit.comment: /* * Number of bytes to read from the random number seed file. This must be * a finite value (because some entropy "files" like /dev/urandom have * an infinite length), but must be large enough to provide enough * entropy to properly seed OpenSSL's PRNG. */

function.name: ossl_keylog_callback

unit.comment: /* * ossl_log_tls12_secret is called by libcurl to make the CLIENT_RANDOMs if the * OpenSSL being used doesn't have native support for doing that. */

function.name: ossl_log_tls12_secret

unit.comment: /* !HAVE_KEYLOG_CALLBACK */

function.name: SSL_ERROR_to_str

unit.comment: /* Return error string for last OpenSSL error */

function.name: ossl_strerror
passwd_callback
rand_enough
ossl_seed
do_file_type

unit.comment: /* * Supply default password to the engine user interface conversation. * The password is passed by OpenSSL engine from ENGINE_load_private_key() * last argument to the ui and can be obtained by UI_get0_user_data(ui) here. */

function.name: ssl_ui_reader
ssl_ui_writer
is_pkcs11_uri

unit.function_decl: static CURLcode ossl_set_engine(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *engine);

function.parameter_list: (SSL_CTX *ctx, const struct curl_blob *blob, int type, const char *key_passwd)

function.parameter_list: (SSL_CTX *ctx, const struct curl_blob *blob, int type, const char *key_passwd)

function.name: SSL_CTX_use_certificate_chain_blob
cert_stuff
Curl_ossl_set_client_cert

unit.comment: /* returns non-zero on failure */

function.name: x509_name_oneline

unit.comment: /** * Global SSL init * * @retval 0 error initializing SSL * @retval 1 SSL initialized successfully */

function.name: ossl_init

unit.comment: /* Global cleanup */

function.name: ossl_cleanup

unit.comment: /* Selects an OpenSSL crypto engine */

function.name: ossl_set_engine

unit.comment: /* Sets engine as default for all SSL operations */

function.name: ossl_set_engine_default

unit.comment: /* Return list of OpenSSL crypto engine names. */

function.name: ossl_engines_list
ossl_close

unit.comment: /* * This function is called to shut down the SSL layer but keep the * socket open (CCC - Clear Command Channel) */

function.name: ossl_shutdown
ossl_session_free

unit.comment: /* * This function is called when the 'data' struct is going away. Close * down everything and free all resources! */

function.name: ossl_close_all
subj_alt_hostcheck

unit.comment:
/* Quote from RFC2818 section 3.1 "Server Identity"
If a subjectAltName extension of type dNSName is present, that MUST be used as the identity. Otherwise, the (most specific) Common Name field in the Subject field of the certificate MUST be used. Although the use of the Common Name is existing practice, it is deprecated and Certification Authorities are encouraged to use the dNSName instead.
Matching is performed using the matching rules specified by [RFC2459]. If more than one identity of a given type is present in the certificate (e.g., more than one dNSName name, a match in any one of the set is considered acceptable.) Names may contain the wildcard character * which is considered to match any single domain name component or component fragment. E.g., *.a.com matches foo.a.com but not bar.foo.a.com. f*.com matches foo.com but not bar.com.
In some cases, the URI is specified as an IP address rather than a hostname. In this case, the iPAddress subjectAltName must be present in the certificate and must exactly match the IP in the URI.
This function is now used from ngtcp2 (QUIC) as well. */

function.name: Curl_ossl_verifyhost
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, struct ssl_peer *peer, X509 *server_cert)

function.name: verifystatus

unit.comment: /* USE_OPENSSL */
/* The SSL_CTRL_SET_MSG_CALLBACK doesn't exist in ancient OpenSSL versions and thus this cannot be done there. */

function.name: ssl_msg_type
tls_rt_type
ossl_trace

unit.comment: /* Check for OpenSSL 1.0.2 which has ALPN support. */

unit.comment: /* * Heavily modified from: * https://www.owasp.org/index.php/Certificate_and_Public_Key_Pinning#OpenSSL */

function.name: ossl_pkp_pin_peer_pubkey
infof_certstack

unit.comment: /* * Get the server cert, verify it and show it, etc., only call failf() if the * 'strict' argument is TRUE as otherwise all this is for informational * purposes only! * * We check certificates to authenticate the server; otherwise we risk * man-in-the-middle attack. */

function.name: servercert
ossl_connect_step3
ossl_connect_common
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, bool nonblocking, bool *done)

function.name: ossl_connect_nonblocking
ossl_connect
ossl_data_pending
ossl_send
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, const void *mem, size_t len, CURLcode *curlcode)

function.name: ossl_recv
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, /* transfer */ char *buf, /* store read data here */ size_t buffersize, /* max amount to read */ CURLcode *curlcode)

function.name: ossl_version

unit.comment: /* can be called with data == NULL */

function.name: ossl_random
ossl_sha256sum
function.parameter_list: (const unsigned char *tmp, /* input */ size_t tmplen, unsigned char *sha256sum /* output */, size_t unused)

function.name: ossl_cert_status_request
ossl_get_internals
ossl_free_multi_ssl_backend_data

unit.decl_stmt:
const struct Curl_ssl Curl_ssl_openssl = { { CURLSSLBACKEND_OPENSSL, "openssl" }, /* info */
SSLSUPP_CA_PATH | SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB | SSLSUPP_CERTINFO | SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY | SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX | #ifdef HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_CIPHERSUITES SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES | #endif SSLSUPP_HTTPS_PROXY,
sizeof(struct ossl_ssl_backend_data),
ossl_init, /* init */ ossl_cleanup, /* cleanup */ ossl_version, /* version */ Curl_none_check_cxn, /* check_cxn */ ossl_shutdown, /* shutdown */ ossl_data_pending, /* data_pending */ ossl_random, /* random */ ossl_cert_status_request, /* cert_status_request */ ossl_connect, /* connect */ ossl_connect_nonblocking, /* connect_nonblocking */ Curl_ssl_adjust_pollset, /* adjust_pollset */ ossl_get_internals, /* get_internals */ ossl_close, /* close_one */ ossl_close_all, /* close_all */ ossl_session_free, /* session_free */ ossl_set_engine, /* set_engine */ ossl_set_engine_default, /* set_engine_default */ ossl_engines_list, /* engines_list */ Curl_none_false_start, /* false_start */ #if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x0090800fL) && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_SHA256) ossl_sha256sum, /* sha256sum */ #else NULL, /* sha256sum */ #endif NULL, /* use of data in this connection */ NULL, /* remote of data from this connection */ ossl_free_multi_ssl_backend_data, /* free_multi_ssl_backend_data */ ossl_recv, /* recv decrypted data */ ossl_send, /* send data to encrypt */ };
unit.comment: /* USE_OPENSSL */
